title: Operator TUI visual view switcher overlay for center viewport
purpose: Extend the center VisualViewport system with a toggleable two-line view switcher/status overlay that shows which center views are usable in the current environment and which views are unavailable, including visible keybinding help and safe default shortcut selection.
goal: The different center views must be switchable at runtime. A compact overlay must show available/working view types in one line and unavailable/impossible view types in a second line. The overlay can be shown/hidden with a default keybinding. Ctrl+M may be supported only when the terminal/input stack can distinguish it from Enter; otherwise F7 is the default. The meaning of the shortcut must be visible in the lower TUI help/status area.

context.user_goal: Die verschiedenen Views in der Mitte sollen umschaltbar sein. Dafür werden zwei Zeilen benötigt: eine Zeile mit erkannten funktionierenden View-Arten, eine zweite Zeile mit View-Arten, die in dieser Umgebung nicht möglich sind. Die Anzeige soll über Ctrl+M ein-/ausblendbar sein; falls Ctrl+M belegt oder ungeeignet ist, soll ein anderer Default verwendet werden. Unten soll eingeblendet werden, was die Tastenkombination bedeutet.

keybinding_observation.ctrl_m_risk: Ctrl+M is usually carriage return / Enter in many terminal input stacks, so it may not be safely distinguishable.
keybinding_observation.optional_alias: Ctrl+M only if keymap probing proves it is distinguishable from Enter in the active terminal/input stack.

desired_overlay_layout.footer_hint: F7 View-Leiste ein/aus | Ctrl+N nächste View | Ctrl+P vorherige View (or configured keys)

design_rules: The overlay is metadata/control UI, not part of the rendered visual frame itself.
The overlay must not steal space from logs/input unpredictably; it must reserve exactly two rows when visible.
Available/unavailable status must be based on actual registry + capability checks, not hardcoded assumptions.
Unavailable views must include a short reason.
Hotkey conflicts must be detected before registering defaults.

milestones.title: Keybinding conflict handling and overlay contract
View capability report and switcher runtime
Two-line overlay rendering and footer help integration

tasks.title: Add visual view switcher keybinding actions
tasks.description: Add keybinding actions for toggling the view switcher overlay and cycling center views.
acceptance_criteria: A new action toggle_visual_view_switcher_overlay exists.
Default key is F7 because Ctrl+M is commonly indistinguishable from Enter in terminal input handling.
Optional Ctrl+M alias is allowed only if keymap/input probing confirms it does not collide with Enter in the active environment.
Actions for next_visual_view and previous_visual_view exist or are explicitly mapped to existing navigation actions without ambiguity.
The configured shortcut appears in footer/shortcut help with a clear label such as 'View-Leiste'.

tasks.title: Implement keybinding conflict detector for visual overlay shortcuts
tasks.description: Detect duplicate or unsafe keybindings during keymap loading.
acceptance_criteria: Duplicate keybindings in the same active area are rejected or reported deterministically.
Ctrl+M is marked unsafe when the input layer maps it to Enter/carriage return.
When Ctrl+M is unsafe, F7 remains the active default without requiring user interaction.
Conflict diagnostics list action name, key, display label and conflicting action.

tasks.title: Define overlay layout contract for two reserved rows
tasks.description: Document where the two-line view switcher appears and how it interacts with the center viewport and footer.
acceptance_criteria: Docs define that the overlay uses exactly two rows when visible.
Docs define whether the overlay is directly below the center viewport or docked at the lower edge of the center area.
Docs state that the visual renderer itself must not draw those two control rows.
Docs include an ASCII mockup showing available and unavailable view lines plus footer hint.

tasks.title: Implement ViewCapabilityReport
tasks.description: Compute which registered views are usable in the current runtime and which are unavailable with reasons.
acceptance_criteria: Report includes available_views and unavailable_views lists.
Each available item includes view_id, display_name, selected flag and optional renderer/adapter requirement summary.
Each unavailable item includes view_id, display_name and a short reason such as missing renderer, unsupported terminal protocol, missing artifact source, missing OpenGL/EGL or disabled by config.
The report is generated from registries, view requirements, renderer availability and terminal capabilities, not from hardcoded view names only.
The report is stable and serializable for logging/debug output.

tasks.title: Add per-view requirements metadata
tasks.description: Each VisualView must expose minimal requirements so the switcher can decide whether it is possible in the active environment.
acceptance_criteria: VisualView metadata includes view_id, display_name, description, required_render_features and optional_runtime_requirements.
Views that work in ANSI fallback declare that explicitly.
Optional high-fidelity views can declare requirements such as raster_frame, image_protocol, opengl_offscreen or artifact_source.
Unavailable status does not require instantiating heavy optional dependencies when metadata is enough.

tasks.title: Implement ViewSwitcher runtime
tasks.description: Provide runtime logic for switching only among currently available center views and for toggling the overlay visibility.
acceptance_criteria: ViewSwitcher can switch by view id and cycle next/previous through available views.
Unavailable views are shown but cannot be selected unless force/debug mode is explicitly enabled.
Switching views clears stale frame/image state where required by the active output adapter.
Overlay visibility is independent from the active center view.
The selected view is reflected in ViewCapabilityReport.

tasks.title: Implement two-line ViewSwitcherOverlay renderer
tasks.description: Render the available/unavailable view lists as two compact rows around the center viewport.
acceptance_criteria: First line starts with a clear label such as 'Views OK:' and lists all currently usable views.
Second line starts with a clear label such as 'Views unavailable:' and lists unavailable views with short reasons.
The active view is visibly marked, for example with brackets, star or prefix.
Long lists are truncated deterministically with a '+N more' indicator instead of wrapping unpredictably.
The overlay uses exactly two rows and does not overwrite the input/footer area.

tasks.title: Integrate overlay toggle into Operator TUI event handling
tasks.description: Wire the overlay toggle keybinding into the interactive TUI without breaking existing input/chat behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Pressing F7 toggles the two-line overlay on/off.
If Ctrl+M is configured and distinguishable, pressing Ctrl+M also toggles the overlay.
If Ctrl+M is not distinguishable from Enter, pressing Enter keeps existing behavior and does not toggle the overlay.
Toggling the overlay recalculates layout so the center viewport stays bounded and surrounding areas remain stable.
The toggle works in normal mode, snake mode and chat-visible mode unless a mode explicitly disables it with a visible reason.

tasks.title: Show bottom help hint for view overlay and view switching
tasks.description: Make the meaning of the key combination visible in the lower shortcut/footer area.
acceptance_criteria: Footer/shortcut area includes 'F7 View-Leiste' or the configured equivalent.
If Ctrl+M alias is active, footer may show 'Ctrl+M/F7 View-Leiste'; if not active, it must not claim Ctrl+M works.
Footer also shows configured next/previous view shortcuts when available.
Shortcut labels are loaded from keybinding config, not duplicated as hardcoded strings in multiple places.

tasks.title: Add tests for keybinding conflict and Ctrl+M fallback behavior
tasks.description: Ensure the default shortcut strategy is deterministic and safe.
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify F7 is selected when Ctrl+M is unsafe or indistinguishable from Enter.
Tests verify Ctrl+M can be enabled as alias only when explicitly supported by the input probe/config.
Tests verify duplicate active-area bindings produce deterministic diagnostics.
Tests verify footer help reflects the actually active keybinding.

tasks.title: Add tests for ViewCapabilityReport and view switching
tasks.description: Verify that available and unavailable views are detected and switched correctly.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover all initial views with full ANSI fallback available.
Tests cover an OpenGL-only view becoming unavailable when OpenGL support is missing.
Tests cover unavailable views being listed with reasons but skipped by next/previous switching.
Tests cover active view marker updating after view switch.

tasks.title: Add snapshot tests for two-line overlay rendering
tasks.description: Keep overlay output stable, compact and predictable.
acceptance_criteria: Snapshot tests verify the exact two-line output for a normal terminal width.
Snapshot tests verify truncation behavior for narrow terminal widths.
Snapshot tests verify unavailable view reasons are included but shortened.
Snapshot tests verify the active view is visibly marked.

summary_notes: Ctrl+M is not currently present in the default keybinding config, but it is unsafe as default because many terminal stacks treat it as Enter.
F7 is the recommended deterministic default for toggling the view switcher overlay.
The overlay must show two rows: available working views and unavailable views with reasons.
The footer must always show the actually active shortcut label, not a hardcoded assumed shortcut.

end_summaries.title: Expected result after completion
items: The user can toggle a compact two-line view switcher/status overlay for the center VisualViewport.
The first line lists all currently usable center views; the second line lists unavailable views with short reasons.
The user can switch between available center views at runtime.
The footer clearly shows the active shortcut, defaulting to F7 and only showing Ctrl+M when it is safe and active.
Unavailable views are visible for transparency but skipped by normal cycling.
